2604.24416.title: Scaling Properties of Continuous Diffusion Spoken Language Models
2604.24416.paper_summary: Speech-only spoken language models (SLMs) lag behind text and text-speech models in performance, with recent discrete autoregressive (AR) SLMs indicating significant computational and data demands to match text models. Since discretizing continuous speech for AR creates bottlenecks, we explore whether continuous diffusion (CD) SLM is more viable. To quantify the SLMs linguistic quality, we introduce the phoneme Jensen-Shannon divergence (pJSD) metric. Our analysis reveals CD SLMs, mirroring AR behavior, exhibit scaling laws for validation loss and pJSD, and show optimal token-to-parameter ratios decreasing as compute scales. However, for the latter, loss becomes insensitive to choice of data and model sizes, showing potential for fast inference. Scaling CD SLMs to 16B parameters with tens of millions of hours of conversational data enables generation of emotive, prosodic, multi-speaker, multilingual speech, though achieving long-form coherence remains a significant challenge.
2604.24416.paper_summary_zh: 語音專用的口語語言模型（SLMs）在性能上落後於文本和文本-語音模型，最近的離散自回歸（AR）SLMs顯示出要與文本模型匹配的計算和數據需求相當高。由於將連續語音進行離散化以用於AR會產生瓶頸，我們探討連續擴散（CD）SLM是否更具可行性。為了量化SLMs的語言質量，我們引入了音素詹森-香農散度（pJSD）指標。我們的分析顯示，CD SLMs模仿AR行為，對驗證損失和pJSD展現出縮放法則，並顯示出隨著計算規模的增長，最佳的標記與參數比率在下降。然而，對於後者，損失對數據和模型大小的選擇變得不敏感，顯示出快速推斷的潛力。將CD SLM擴展到160億個參數，並使用數千萬小時的對話數據，可以生成情感豐富的、韻律化的、多說話者的、多語言的語音，儘管實現長篇一致性仍然是一個重大挑戰。
2604.24416.author: Jason Ramapuram et.al.
2604.24416.authors: Jason Ramapuram, Eeshan Gunesh Dhekane, Amitis Shidani, Dan Busbridge, Bogdan Mazoure, Zijin Gu, Russ Webb, Tatiana Likhomanenko, Navdeep Jaitly
